mentions.note: Mistaken identity: never fundamentally broken despite stock lagging S&P 500 (up only 5% vs. 16% S&P, 20% Nasdaq in 2025). AWS accelerating >20%, retail strong with double-digit international growth, advertising gravy, Anthropic investment driving retail robotics. Now playing catch-up; up ~5% in past three sessions.
Retail and tech play; diversifies portfolio across sectors.
Worst MAG7 performer last year but still up slightly YTD; more defensive positioning; holding up better than peers.
Historic Cramer Fang+ holding; facing temporary money outflows to storage plays but Cramer believes capital will return to Magnificent 7 as storage rally peaks.
Controversial stock; formerly roared on earnings but now gets faded on strength and sells off on weakness; owns for Charitable Trust but has not been good returner; let earnings speak.
Reports this week; optimistic but wary market already reached verdict. Being beaten by Walmart on web services (AWS), by everyone on Prime. Doesn't get respect deserved; other stocks do.

$200B capex (vs 146.6B expected) for AI, retail, Starlink; AWS strong but capex burden and weak OpInc guidance dominated; down >5%; requires faith in Andy Jassny execution; holding CBC Investing Club but hard stock; don't pound table.
AWS fears of Anthropic disruption overblown; stock broke out of negative range, rallied ~$8 (4%). Market recognizing AWS is not at risk from AI agents; strong infrastructure tailwind ahead.
$50B commitment to OpenAI fundraise; strategic partnership on stateful runtime product and Frontier deployment; cloud infrastructure critical to AI scaling.
Naysayers warned consumer slowdown will hurt Prime; Cramer knows Amazon 'tight as drum,' doesn't leak retail intel; held position—correct call on tech company opacity.

AWS $161B revenue, $200B 2026 CapEx with pre-committed customer revenue, proprietary chips 'on fire,' satellite/drone logistics transforming logistics. Jassy's letter reassuring; AI ROI thesis sound. Cramer tempted to buy more on dips despite already holding low-cost-basis position.
Jennifer's portfolio: retail but also lots of tech; amazing conglomerate; keep it; good diversifying position.
Left for dead on AWS slowdown, retail weakness, Jassy vs. Bezos concerns; CEO letter clarifying direction sparked 25-point move; satellite acquisition added 8 points; no disaster materialized.
Huge Nvidia customer despite touting own chips; not moving away from Nvidia; continued reliance on Nvidia for AI infrastructure.
AWS (Amazon Web Services) critical infrastructure layer in AI build-out; announced major contract with Anthropic. Beneficiary of data center capex wave.
Stock soaring since CEO Andy Jassy's vision letter. Now earnings pressure; could be tall order but sticking with it. Will buy on pullback if it comes.
Reports Wednesday; key to gauge if hyperscalers will pause spending or see ROI from AI capex. Watch capex guidance on call.
Big Four earnings tomorrow (Wed); watch capex vs. revenue growth; Microsoft serial underperformer in recent tech quarters; market may need disappointment.
AWS growth 28% (insanely high); CEO Andy Jassy from that division. Cloud business fantastic; may need more power/compute, hinting at shortage narrative.

17% revenue growth, 75% EPS growth, AWS +28% (best in 4 years), custom chip business at $20B run rate, operating income +30%; guidance mixed on operating income; parabolic move consolidating, CEO Jassy best explainer of data center ROI; stock should go higher but move overstretched.
Charitable trust holding; ultimate trade-down play in a crimped consumer environment; computer-driven economy anchor stock with structural insulation from oil and rate shocks.

AWS AI run-rate at $15B (260x the original 3-year AWS pace) and growing triple digits; Trainium/Graviton chip business worth $50B+ at rack-sale prices; Supply Chain Services, Amazon Leo, and healthcare add multiple new TAM vectors.
Amazon Web Services is one of two main public data center winners; ensured years of colossal profits from AI infrastructure capital spending.
Massive capex commitment essential to retain AI customer contracts (Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta, TikTok); AWS winning clients at accelerating pace.
Magnificent Seven member owned by Cramer's charitable trust. Original FANG stock; demonstrated ability to scale despite heavy borrowing; e-commerce and cloud dominance; secular megatrend.
Important AI capabilities; mentioned as context for tech dominance but not specifically recommended for action.
Cramer is 'huge believer'—Prime, AWS, advertising all thriving. Own AI chips hold value equal to Nvidia's at lower cost; likely reason Nvidia stalled while Amazon surged.
Has own chips; pointedly absent from Jensen's winners list but Cramer likes the trio; Berkshire Hathaway buying $10B in equity raise signals confidence; deep enterprise relationship.
Heavy data center spending may necessitate equity raising similar to Microsoft; monitor for secondary offering announcements that would pressure stock and shareholders.
